experiences.intentions: Active piece is Z near the top in its spawn orientation, with an empty board and no immediate threats. Goal is to place it efficiently while setting up for future T, S, I, L pieces; early game favors getting pieces down quickly and keeping the stack low. Candidate moves: left/right only shift the Z with no immediate benefit, rotation changes shape but is unnecessary on an empty board, soft_drop advances only one row, while hard_drop safely locks it to the bottom and preserves a flat low stack. Best action is to hard drop.

The stack below is low-to-mid with a relatively flat surface around columns 4-7, and there is no immediate top-out threat, so the main goal is to improve placement for future T, I, Z, L pieces while avoiding creating overhangs. Among candidates, hard_drop/soft_drop commit too early from a poor central spawn, rotations do not yet create a clearly superior landing at this height, and right would drift toward the S/Z clutter on the right. Moving left preserves flexibility and heads toward a cleaner tuck opportunity.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T near the top-center in rotation 1, with a relatively flat area and no immediate danger since the stack is low. Goal is to place the T efficiently while keeping the center open for upcoming I/Z/L/T pieces. Candidate actions: left/right just reposition without committing; rotate changes shape but current orientation already fits cleanly above the center-right contours; hard_drop would likely lock too early without fine positioning. Soft_drop advances toward placement while preserving flexibility next turn, so it best pursues safe development now.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is an I tetromino currently horizontal near the top at row 3, columns 4-7. The board surface below is uneven, with a notable vertical well around column 5 and some future flexibility needed. Immediate goal is to place the I efficiently without creating overhangs; rotating vertical now aligns it over the central channel and preserves lateral options for a later drop. Left/right while horizontal offers less commitment, and dropping horizontally this early would likely rest high on bumps. So the best setup move is to rotate clockwise into vertical orientation.
